scope: Phase-specific cross-cutting integration scenarios: multi-owner trees, failed accepts, reset behavior, status views, lazy evaluation properties.

tests.name: integration: multi-owner story — create, transfer ownership, recipient acts, original blocked
integration: §14 bullet 4 stranded U-owned demonstration end-to-end
integration: cross-tree rename — succeeds when no clash, 409s when target name conflicts in another tree
integration: reset between flows wipes everything, ID counter restarts
integration: a delete blocked by precondition fails 409, then Alice fixes the shape and the retry succeeds
Appendix A.1 end-to-end via accept-cascade
Appendix A.2 end-to-end with case-3 invalidation and dismiss
§14 collaborative deletion: U-owned shared taxon blocks delete until detached via proposals
§14 ownership reassignment reroutes promotion-time reviewer
§14 cascade-delete halts at other-owned; queued change under detached taxon becomes case-3 invalid
reset across Phase-7 lifecycle: clears cascade, case-3 invalid, and case-2 auto-dismissed state
proposer view shows `invalid` for all three §11.4 cases, identically
/queue includes case-3 invalid entries but NOT case-1 / case-2
successful cascade leaves queue clean of the cascade's accepted set
Phase 2 integration: bootstrap flow
fresh server → reset → register → list → identity classifies caller
Phase 2 integration: register-then-reset reclassifies identity
registered caller becomes unregistered after POST /reset
Phase 2 integration: cross-casing round trip
Caller.username is the registry's stored casing on read
Phase 2 integration: Phase 1 regressions still hold
GET /health is unaffected by the identity middleware
unknown route still 404s with the standard envelope
Phase 3 integration: full lifecycle
register → seed → read each endpoint → reset → reads empty
Phase 3 integration: shared:true surfaces end-to-end
a shared taxon shows shared:true via both /taxa and the containing /trees responses
Phase 4 integration: two-user lifecycle
register → create → add edge → PATCH owner → DELETE
Phase 4 integration: multi-step collaborative delete (§14)
blocked shared delete becomes unblocked after detaching the share
Phase 4 integration: §11.1 concurrent serialization
ten concurrent POST /taxa requests produce ten distinct taxa with sequential IDs
concurrent PATCHes on the same taxon serialize: final name is one of the two
Phase 6 cross-tree validation end-to-end
rename of a shared taxon clashing in the OTHER tree → 409 with that tree's rootId
add-graft of a taxon causing a clash in the grafted-into tree → 409
Phase 6 failed-accept persistence end-to-end
intervening direct action makes the proposal invalid; accept fails; change is invalid in queue
Phase 6 multi-owner integration
a proposal that touches taxa owned by two users routes each change to its respective owner
Bob accepts and Carol rejects independent changes in the same proposal
Phase 6 reject cascade end-to-end
rejecting an outer add-create cascades to its nested rename via decisionDeps
Phase 6 reset composition
after accept/reject/failed-accept, POST /reset returns proposal-store and taxa-store to fresh-boot
Phase 6 review loop end-to-end
Alice proposes, Bob accepts each change in dependency order
Phase 7 — case 2: same-reviewer accept auto-dismisses other queued
Bob's accept of X invalidates and auto-dismisses Bob's other queued Y
Bob's accept-cascade auto-dismisses Bob's other queued change
Phase 7 — case 3: cross-reviewer accept marks but keeps queued
Bob's accept invalidates Carol's queued change as case 3
Phase 7 — case 3: direct actions trigger case 3
direct rename by Alice invalidates Bob's queued change as case 3
direct delete by Bob (the reviewer) is case 3, not case 2
Phase 7 — case-1 cascade from non-reject invalidation
direct action that invalidates an add-create cascades case-1 to its latent descendants
case-2 invalidation of add-create cascades to its descendants
Phase 7 — lazy evaluation: reads do not auto-mutate
GET /proposals/{id} does not transition a queued change to invalid
GET /queue does not auto-dismiss invalid-in-queue changes
Proposer's view shows the persisted disposition
Phase 7 — §14 properties
moves are non-atomic (detach + add as separate proposals)
ownership reassignment is unilateral and does not invalidate queued changes (sticky routing)
cascade deletion halts at others' taxa (Phase 4 property reaffirmed under Phase 7)
Phase 7 — reset after each new state path
reset after successful cascade
reset after rolled-back cascade
reset after case-1 cascade (reject of nested add-create)
reset after explicit dismiss
omnibus: reset after a sequence of operations
